content.problemStatement:
One day, Tanya was studying graph theory. She is very inquisitive, so the following problem soon came to her mind.

Find the number of undirected unweighted connected simple graphs with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges, such that for each iii (2≤i≤N2 \le i \le N2≤i≤N), the shortest path from vertex 111 to vertex iii is unique and its length is equal to AiA_iAi​. In other words, for each iii (2≤i≤N2 \le i \le N2≤i≤N), there should be exactly one path with length AiA_iAi​ between vertices 111 and iii, and there should be no paths with smaller length between these vertices.

Two graphs with NNN vertices are distinct if we can find two vertices uuu and vvv such that there is an edge between these vertices in one graph, but not in the other graph.

Since the answer could be very large, compute it modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7).
constraints: 1≤Ai≤N−11 \le A_i \le N-11≤Ai​≤N−1 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains N−1N - 1N−1 space-separated integers A2,A3,…,ANA_2, A_3, \ldots, A_NA2​,A3​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of graphs modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
